frameworks.name: information-flow account of prefrontal function
frameworks.one_line: describe PF subregions by input-to-output transformations rather than psychological labels

frameworks.name: psychological-labelling of PF areas
frameworks.one_line: traditional approach assigning cognitive constructs to PF subregions

frameworks.name: abstract rule / transformation encoding
frameworks.one_line: mid-dorsal and ventral PF encode action- and object-invariant transformations

core_question: What is the right level of description for the functions of the prefrontal cortex subregions?
key_assumptions: PF function is best understood by characterising input-output transformations
PF subregions are functionally specialised by the kind of transformation they implement
abstract transformations generalise across specific actions or objects
manipulations: contrast across caudal, mid-dorsal, and ventral PF subregions
contrast between attention/short-term memory, sequence-order, and object/sound inputs
key_findings: caudal PF maps attended or remembered input to eye-movement targets
mid-dorsal PF maps order of objects or actions to sequential eye and hand movement targets
ventral PF maps object or sound input to prospective associated object or sound activity
mid-dorsal and ventral PF encode abstract transformations invariant to specific actions or objects
interpretations: PF function should be described by flow of information rather than psychological labels
abstract transformation coding supports generalisation to novel problems sharing logic
